perturbation.original_text: With respect to any oral or visual communication or other intangible information which a Disclosing Party would like to be treated as Confidential Information under this Agreement, the Disclosing Party shall notify Recipient of such fact at the time of disclosure and within fiftee n (15) days thereafter, Disclosing Party shall send Recipient a written memorandum outlining the information deemed to be Confidential Information. Such memorandum shall be marked “Confidential.”
perturbation.changed_text: With respect to any oral or visual communication or other intangible information which a Disclosing Party would like to be treated as Confidential Information under this Agreement, the Disclosing Party shall notify Recipient of such fact at the time of disclosure; sending a written memorandum outlining the information is at the Disclosing Party's discretion. Such memorandum may be marked “Confidential.”
perturbation.explanation: By changing the requirement to send a written memorandum within 15 days to being at the disclosing party's discretion, and making the marking of the memorandum optional in Section 1(c), we create an in-text contradiction with other sections that reference confidential information being marked. It is now unclear which information is actually protected under the NDA and it's contradicting with the obligation of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A Recipient of Confidential Information under this Agreement shall use the Confidential Information only for the purpose of evaluating a research relationship between the Parties and shall protect such Confidential Information from disclosure to others, using the same degree of care used to protect its own confidentiality or proprietary information of like importance. If necessary to effectuate the furthering of a potential research relationship, Recipient may disclose Confidential Information received under this Agreement to employees and/or cons ultants with a need to know, provided that any consultants are bound to protect such Confidential Information from unauthorized use and disclosure under the terms of a written agreement. Confidential Information shall not otherwise be disclosed to any thi rd party without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party. Neither Party shall use the Confidential Information of the other, in whole or in part, except as permitted under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates a written memorandum outlining confidential information within 15 days, marked 'Confidential'. The changed text makes sending the memorandum discretionary and marking it optional. The contradicted text refers to 'Confidential Information' needing protection and possible disclosure only to employees/consultants under written agreement. By making the memorandum discretionary, it's unclear which information is actually considered confidential, creating a conflict. Without a mandatory, marked memorandum, it's hard to determine what qualifies as 'Confidential Information' under the contract, thus contradicting the requirement to protect it and potentially disclose it only under specific conditions. Therefore, it is creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement (including without limitation information in computer software or held in electronic storage media) shall be and remain in the property of Disclosing Party. All such information in tangible form shall be returned to Disclosing Party promptly upon written request by Disclosing Party or the termination or expiration of this Agreement, whichever occurs first, and shall not thereafter be retained in any form by Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: All tangible Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement (including without limitation information in computer software or held in electronic storage media) shall be and remain in the property of Disclosing Party. All such information in tangible form shall be returned to Disclosing Party within 90 days of written request by Disclosing Party, even after the termination or expiration of this Agreement, and may be retained thereafter in any form by Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: By limiting the clause to only tangible information, changing the deadline to 90 days of written notice, introducing retention post-termination in Section 4, we create an in-text contradiction with Section 5, which dictates the overall duration of confidentiality. It is now unclear if the obligation for confidentiality continues for 5 years according to Section 5, or if Recipient may retain and potentially use the information after the agreement's expiration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient shall maintain in confidence and shall not disclose to any person not a party hereto, unless permitted to do so under Section 2, or use or exploit in any way, without the Disclosing Party’s written agreement, any Confidential Information for a period of five (5) years from the date of disclosure of such information, unless such information ceases to be Confidential Information prior to the end of such five -year period through no fault of Recipient, or Recipient an d Disclosing Party enter into a written agreement authorizing same.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original agreement regarding confidential information. Specifically, it limits the clause to 'tangible' information, extends the return deadline to 90 days, and allows retention post-termination. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which requires maintaining confidentiality for five years from disclosure. The changed text now allows retention and potential use of information after the agreement's expiration, conflicting with the five-year confidentiality period specified in the contradicted section. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, where different sections provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement will be one (1) year from the Effective Date, unless it is terminated earlier by either party providing the other party with at least thirty (30) days prior written notice, or unless it is extended as mutually agreed upon by the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement will be perpetually, from the Effective Date, and it can only be terminated with mutual agreement from both parties. This term supersedes any other terms related to agreement termination.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the term and termination conditions of the agreement to be perpetual unless mutually agreed upon for termination in Section 15, there is an in-text contradiction with Section 4, where the confidential information should be returned after the termination of the agreement, this leads to confusion and uncertainty of the agreement conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement (including without limitation information in computer software or held in electronic storage media) shall be and remain in the property of Disclosing Party. All such information in tangible form shall be returned to Disclosing Party promptly upon written request by Disclosing Party or the termination or expiration of this Agreement, whichever occurs first, and shall not thereafter be retained in any form by Recipient. In lieu of return, such infor mation may be destroyed by the Recipient provided any such destruction shall be certified in writing to the Disclosing Party by one of Recipient’s duly authorized officers. No intellectual property rights, including but not limited to, licenses or rights under any patent, copyright, trademark or trade secret, are granted or are to be implied by this Agreement. Neither Party is obligated under this Agreement to purchase from or provide to the other Party any service or product or enter into any agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the agreement's term to 'perpetually' and requires mutual agreement for termination. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that confidential information should be returned upon the termination or expiration of the agreement. Since the agreement is now perpetual (unless both parties agree to terminate), there is uncertainty about when the confidential information should be returned, creating a strong in-text contradiction as Section 4 relies on a specific term to occur. This aligns with the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.
